LAW AND MOTION, 301, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS- DEFENDANT RENATO SALOMONE'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT ON FILE. JUDGE: RONALD EVANS QUIDACHAY, REPORTER: ROBERT BALIAN, CSR # 5220 301/REQ

DISCOVERY 612, DEFENDANT MATTHEW GERTZOG'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS IT SEEKS AN ORDER RELIEVING DEFENDANT FROM RESPONDING TO REQUEST FOR ADMISSION NOS. 6-35 AS THEY ARE UNDULY BURDENSOME. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS IT SEEKS AN ORDER RELIEVING DEFENDANT FROM RESPONDING TO THE SECOND SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. ALL REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT RENATO SALOMONE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT OF MATTHEW GERTZOG IS GRANTED. THERE IS NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER THE CONDUCT ON WHICH CROSS-COMPLAINT'S INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CAUSE OF ACTION IS BASED IS ABSOLUTELY PRIVILEGED UNDER CIVIL CODE SECTION 47 (B). MOVING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

Notice Of Motion For Award Of Attorney Fees And Costs As Monetary Sanctions On Account Of Bseless X-Complaint, And First Amended X-Complaint Of Matthew Gertzog
Off Calendar Dec-30-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JESSE C. RALPH, TEL. NO. 776-1826 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. SCOTT R. LOVERNICK OF SCHWARTZ & CERA LLP, TEL. NO. 956-2600 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT MATTHEW GERTZOG.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF AND CROSS-DEFENDANT RENATO SALOMONE'S Motion For Award Of Attorney Fees And Costs As Monetary Sanctions On Account Of Baseless Cross-Complaint, And First Amended Cross-Complaint Of Matthew Gertzog IS DENIED. CROSS-COMPLAINT DID NOT VIOLATE SECTION 128.7 (b)(1) and (2). Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Paul H. Alvarado, Reporter: Paula Shi, CSR # 13358

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-29-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT RENATO SALOMONE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT OF MATTHEW GERTZOG IS GRANTED. THERE IS NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER THE CONDUCT ON WHICH CROSS-COMPLAINT'S INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CAUSE OF ACTION IS BASED IS ABSOLUTELY PRIVILEGED UNDER CIVIL CODE SECTION 47 (b). Moving party is to submit a proposed form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(g). Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR # 8189 =301/PJB

MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES- ATTY. JESSE C. RALPH, TEL. NO. 776-1826 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY;
ATTY. SCOTT R. LOVERNICK OF THE SCHWARTZ & CERA LLP, TEL. NO. 956-2600 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT RENATO SALOMONE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT OF MATTHEW GERTZOG IS OVERRULED WITH 10 DAYS TO ANSWER WHETHER THE PRELITIGATION LETTER WAS SENT IN GOOD FAITH IS A FACTUAL ISSUE THAT CANNOT BE DETERMINED ON DEMURRER. Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order And Req. For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-26-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Defendant Matthew Gertzog's Motion For Protective Order is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as it seeks an order relieving Defendant from responding to Request For Admission Nos. 6-35 as they are unduly burdensome. The motion is denied as it seeks an order relieving Defendant from responding to the second set of special interrogatories. All requests for sanctions are denied. If oral argument is requested, appear at 10:30 a.m. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED. (EAH/612)
